Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.json

Class JsonTokenWriter

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.JsonTokenWriter
All Implemented Interfaces:
Closeable, Flushable, AutoCloseable, RecordWriter, TokenWriter
public class JsonTokenWriter extends Object implements TokenWriter
Reference implementation of the public TokenWriter surface for the JSON format.

Emits RFC-8259-compliant JSON. Output style is driven by a small JsonTokenWriter.Settings bundle that mirrors the structurally-applicable subset of JsonSerializer.Builder's formatting flags: useWhitespace, maxIndent, quoteChar, escapeSolidus, and trimStrings. When opened via JsonSerializer.serializeTokens(Object) the writer inherits the settings from the calling serializer; constructing the writer directly via JsonTokenWriter(Writer) uses JsonTokenWriter.Settings.DEFAULT (compact, double-quoted, RFC-8259 strict).

Notes:
  • This class is not thread safe.
  • The writer tracks just enough state to insert structural separators (, between array/object members and : between field names and values). It does not validate event ordering — emitting field twice in a row, for instance, throws.
  • The writer is purely structural; object swaps and @Schema annotations have no effect. Use the POJO databind path on JsonSerializer for those.

  • Constructor Details

    • JsonTokenWriter

      public JsonTokenWriter(Writer out)
      Constructor for a Writer target with default JsonTokenWriter.Settings.
      Parameters:
      out - The writer to emit JSON to. Must not be null.

    • JsonTokenWriter

      public JsonTokenWriter(Writer out, JsonTokenWriter.Settings settings)
      Constructor for a Writer target with explicit JsonTokenWriter.Settings.
      Parameters:
      out - The writer to emit JSON to. Must not be null.
      settings - The output-formatting settings. Must not be null.

    • object

      public TokenWriter object(Object value) throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: TokenWriter
      Emits an arbitrary POJO through the format's structural events.

      The value is walked via PojoWalker: nulls / scalars / collections / arrays / maps / beans are introspected and re-emitted through this writer's TokenWriter.startObject(), TokenWriter.fieldName(String), TokenWriter.string(String), TokenWriter.number(long), TokenWriter.bool(boolean), TokenWriter.nil(), and TokenWriter.binary(byte[]) methods. Per-format encoding (quoting, indentation, newlines, etc.) is whatever this writer is configured for.

      Cursor positioning: the writer must be in a state where a value emit is legal — at root, after a TokenWriter.fieldName(String) call, or inside an array. Calling object(...) from inside an object body without a preceding field name throws IllegalStateException, same as calling TokenWriter.string(String) would.

      Default behavior: the default implementation throws UnsupportedOperationException. Format-specific token writers override this by delegating to PojoWalker.walk(TokenWriter, Object).

      Swaps and @Schema: this method is purely structural. Object swaps and schema annotations are NOT applied at the token layer — use the POJO databind path on Serializer.serialize(Object) for those.

      Specified by:
      object in interface TokenWriter
      Parameters:
      value - The value to emit. May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the underlying stream fails.

    • settingsAccess

      public JsonTokenWriter.Settings settingsAccess()
      Returns the JsonTokenWriter.Settings this writer was configured with.

      Exposed for delegating wrappers (e.g. JsonlTokenWriter) that need to read the underlying writer's walk options when implementing object(Object).

      Returns:
      The settings. Never null.

    • binary

      public TokenWriter binary(byte[] value) throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: TokenWriter
      Emits a TokenType.VALUE_BINARY event.

      Text formats normally encode the value as base64-encoded TokenType.VALUE_STRING; the exact encoding is format-defined.

      Specified by:
      binary in interface TokenWriter
      Parameters:
      value - The value. May be null, in which case a TokenType.VALUE_NULL event is emitted instead.
      Returns:
      This object.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred writing to the underlying stream.

    • flush

      public void flush() throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: TokenWriter
      Flushes any buffered output to the underlying stream.
      Specified by:
      flush in interface Flushable
      Specified by:
      flush in interface RecordWriter
      Specified by:
      flush in interface TokenWriter
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred flushing the underlying stream.

    • close

      public void close() throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: TokenWriter
      Closes the writer, flushing any buffered output first.

      Implementations should be idempotent — closing a closed writer is a no-op.

      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface Closeable
      Specified by:
      close in interface RecordWriter
      Specified by:
      close in interface TokenWriter
      Throws:
      IOException

    • forOutput

      public static JsonTokenWriter forOutput(Object output, JsonTokenWriter.Settings settings) throws IOException
      Internal factory used by JsonSerializer.serializeTokens(Object). Coerces the supported output types (Writer / OutputStream / File / StringBuilder) into a Writer the JSON encoder can target.
      Parameters:
      output - The output object.
      settings - The output-formatting settings.
      Returns:
      A new JsonTokenWriter writing to the resolved Writer.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the output type is not supported or could not be opened.
